description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in what appears to be thatch or weathered wooden shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontally stacked logs, with visible log ends at the corners and vertical support beams. The roof overhangs slightly on all sides, providing some protection from the elements. There is a single, centered doorway on one long side, with no visible windows or additional doors on any facade. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, hand-built structure suitable for rural or forested environments. The cabin's earthy tones and natural materials blend seamlessly with outdoor settings.
description: A rustic log cabin with a steep gable thatched roof, log walls, and a single centered doorway. No windows or porches are visible, emphasizing a simple, traditional design.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from above one long side, showing the full length of the thatched gable roof and the log wall beneath, with no visible openings.
description_view_1: The cabin is viewed from the gable end, highlighting the triangular roof profile and the stacked log construction at the end wall.
description_view_2: This view shows the opposite long side, featuring a centered doorway in the log wall beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is visible, again showing the triangular roof and log end details, with no openings.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with thatched or weathered wooden shingle covering and moderate overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: A single exterior door is centered on one long side of the cabin
